commit.message:
docs(plans): close roadmap and clean bus docs

Summary:
- move the umbrella refactor roadmap to completed and leave no active
  execution plans until new focused work is promoted from debt
- refresh the tech debt tracker so it reflects the landed domain
  timestamp and image-size guard work plus the remaining validation gaps
- make EtherCAT.Bus.t() opaque so generated docs stop referencing the
  hidden InFlight and Submission modules

Rationale:
- the roadmap work for the current library line is finished, so keeping
  it active was stale and hid the real remaining work in broad pending
  phases
- Bus docs should not leak internal scheduler structs through a public
  type, and ExDoc was correctly warning about that boundary

Validation:
- mix test
- mix docs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs(plans): close runtime module decomposition

Summary:
- move the runtime module decomposition plan to completed and mark
  phase 4 complete in the umbrella roadmap
- update AGENTS, WORKFLOW, ARCHITECTURE, and docs/index to point at
  the real module-doc source files instead of stale lib/*.md paths
- refresh the completed plan text so it reflects the landed module
  names and final decomposition outcome

Rationale:
- the structural refactor is finished, so keeping the plan active and
  leaving stale entry paths in the live docs was misleading
- closing the plan now makes the current runtime shape the baseline for
  future cleanup instead of treating landed work as still pending

Validation:
- mix test
- mix docs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

完善吸积盘动画与纹理生成控制

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor(slave): tighten shell event handling

Summary:
- make OP enter actions declarative and remove the redundant latch poll
  re-check in the state handler
- move cached domain offset reuse into ProcessData and retarget the
  reconnect tests to that boundary
- keep the transition hook shape unchanged while trimming the FSM shell

Rationale:
- keep state shell code focused on routing instead of procedural action
  assembly and reconnect-cache details
- align the cached offset helper with the process-data reconnect logic
  without inventing extra abstraction around the transition hook

Validation:
- mix format
- mix test test/ethercat/slave_test.exs
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor(domain): clarify cycle fault semantics

Summary:
- split invalid cycle responses from consecutive transport misses
- document that cycle-missed telemetry covers both fault classes
- simplify small domain helpers in calls and image modules

Rationale:
- make the WKC mismatch vs transport miss distinction explicit in the
  domain loop instead of hiding it in nearly identical helpers
- reduce misleading helper shapes and small warning workarounds that
  made the domain runtime harder to read

Validation:
- mix format
- mix test test/ethercat/domain_test.exs
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor(slave): clarify bootstrap and reconfigure flow

Summary:
- name the mailbox SyncManager control bytes and flatten the bootstrap PREOP flow
- split the PREOP reconfigure policies and remove small transition/health allocation leaks

Rationale:
- make the slave runtime helpers easier to read without changing behavior
- reflect the review feedback directly in the extracted slave modules

Validation:
- mix format
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor(domain): extract runtime helpers

Summary:
- extract domain call handling, cycle execution, process-image access, and status snapshots into dedicated modules
- keep EtherCAT.Domain focused on public API, state entry, and gen_statem routing

Rationale:
- align the domain runtime structure with the same shell-and-subsystems model used for Slave
- separate ETS image concerns from cycle control so the hot path is easier to reason about

Validation:
- mix format
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor(slave): reduce module to state shell

Summary:
- extract call handling, configuration flow, output staging, and status assembly into dedicated slave helpers
- keep EtherCAT.Slave focused on public API, gen_statem routing, and lifecycle shell logic

Rationale:
- finish the slave decomposition without hiding protocol behavior behind one large module
- stop at the point where further extraction would only move shell code around

Validation:
- mix format
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor(slave): extract runtime subsystems

Summary:
- extract bootstrap, transition, process-data, mailbox, DC, and health flows into dedicated modules

- keep EtherCAT.Slave focused on state coordination, public API, and event routing
Rationale:

- reduce the god-object pressure in slave.ex without changing lifecycle behavior
- align the runtime structure more closely with the protocol areas in the spec reference index
Validation:

- mix test test/ethercat/slave_test.exs
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor(runtime): extract master workflows and slave signals

Summary:
- extract Master startup, activation, and recovery workflows into
  dedicated helper modules
- extract Slave signal subscription, input read, and input dispatch
  helpers into EtherCAT.Slave.Signals
- reduce inline coordinator logic in master.ex and runtime signal
  plumbing in slave.ex

Rationale:
- continue Phase 4 by shrinking the largest mixed-concern runtime
  modules without changing protocol behavior
- keep state-machine shells focused on event routing and live state
  ownership
- make the remaining Slave bootstrap, transition, and process-data
  extractions easier to land in smaller steps

Validation:
- mix format
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor(master): extract status and session helpers

Summary:
- add EtherCAT.Master.Status for phase, reply, and runtime query assembly
- add EtherCAT.Master.Session for teardown ordering and monitor cleanup
- add the runtime module decomposition plan and align the roadmap to it

Rationale:
- start Phase 4 by trimming pure coordinator logic out of EtherCAT.Master
- keep the refactor structural so runtime semantics stay unchanged
- make the next startup and recovery extractions fit an explicit plan

Validation:
- mix format
- mix test test/ethercat/master_test.exs
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor(homey): harden shared extension read preflight

- extract shared extension defined-value-id preflight helper for cover/thermostat/siren read pipelines
- remove duplicated bridge/node/client/value-id resolution paths while preserving deterministic reason semantics
- sync sprint and extension plan docs to current implemented vertical state and next tasks

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
chore: align Symphony-claude branding and green Elixir CI

Summary:
- rename public-facing docs and clone examples to Symphony-claude
- refresh Elixir backend docs to match the standalone repository branding
- fix Credo and Dialyzer issues in agent runtime/parser modules
- extend config and test helpers for pipeline validation coverage
- align coverage ignores with the repo's existing runtime coverage policy

Rationale:
- the standalone GitHub repo should present a consistent project identity
- Elixir CI was blocked by lint, type, coverage, and flaky timing test issues
- keeping the runtime baseline green makes the public repo easier to trust

Tests:
- make -C elixir all MIX='mise exec -- mix'

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
docs: refresh public project and runtime guides

Summary:
- rewrite the root README for external users and adoption context
- add a standalone introduction doc for repository overview and usage model
- update the Elixir runtime docs and Claude compatibility notes

Rationale:
- the existing docs were too sparse for external operators evaluating the repo
- backend documentation was stale relative to the current Claude integration path
- publishing to GitHub needs a clearer project introduction and setup story

Tests:
- make -C elixir all MIX=misе exec -- mix (fails on pre-existing Credo issues in agent runtime files unrelated to this docs-only change)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(requirements): pin black to an available release (#209)

Summary:
- replace the invalid black 25.12.0 pin with black 25.11.0
- keep the requirements change scoped to the broken dependency entry

Rationale:
- pip install -r requirements.txt failed because black 25.12.0 is not
  published on PyPI
- black 25.11.0 resolves cleanly in a fresh Python 3.13 environment,
  which matches the repo's declared runtime

Tests:
- source venv/bin/activate && pip install -r requirements.txt
  (failed before the fix with No matching distribution found for
  black==25.12.0)
- test ! -e env && /opt/homebrew/bin/python3.13 -m venv env && source
  env/bin/activate && python --version && pip --version && pip install
  -r requirements.txt
- source env/bin/activate && python - <<'PY' ...
  (confirmed black=25.11.0 and click=8.3.1)
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(agent-browser): Add standalone agent-browser plugin package (#76)

Add a standalone `@sentry/junior-agent-browser` plugin package with docs
and release wiring, and include post-rebase stabilization changes needed
to keep this branch consistently green.

The plugin packaging change keeps browser automation integration
isolated as a distributable capability. The stabilization commit adds
root-level `simple-git-hooks` + `lint-staged` formatting and removes
transient `.next/dev` type artifacts from the example app's tracked
typecheck surface to prevent recurring local churn and typecheck
failures.

I considered splitting the stabilization commit into a follow-up PR, but
keeping it here keeps this rebased branch reviewable and merge-ready
after syncing with `main`.

This PR remains paired with PR #77 for broader runtime/snapshot work
outside the standalone agent-browser package scope.

---------

Co-authored-by: GPT-5 Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: GPT-5 Codex <noreply@openai.com>

fix(api): consolidate runtime routes into a single root function (#21)

Summary:
- Added `api/[...route].ts` as a consolidated dispatcher for runtime
  endpoints: health, agent status/run, checkout, portal, and stripe
  webhook.
- Inlined stable self-contained implementations for these routes in
  the catch-all handler.
- Removed standalone root function files for health, agent status,
  agent run, checkout, and portal.

Rationale:
- Recent releases consistently failed when adding one more root
  function endpoint, while equal-count replacements deployed cleanly.
- Consolidating multiple endpoints into one function keeps behavior
  available without increasing function count.
- This unblocks publication of `/api/webhooks/stripe` while retaining
  the already shipped API behavior patterns.

Tests:
- npm run build

Co-authored-by: dongchangjin <dongchangjin@ccbscf.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Pr/html sanitization phase1 (#2)

* Relax HTML sanitization for safer email fidelity

Document the phased sanitization plan and preserve more sender-intended presentation by allowing sanitized inline CSS and common email layout attributes while keeping remote content and active elements blocked.

Co-authored-by: Codex (GPT-5.4) <codex@openai.com>

* Block CSS image fetch functions in sanitizer

Tighten the inline CSS policy by disallowing background shorthand and rejecting image-set/image CSS values so remote resources cannot be fetched through sanitized styles.

Co-authored-by: Codex (GPT-5.4) <codex@openai.com>

* Version sanitized HTML policy and lazy upgrade

Add a persisted sanitized HTML version, migrate existing rows to version 1, and lazily re-sanitize cached message bodies to policy version 2 on first access while keeping the updated sanitizer behavior and docs aligned.

Co-authored-by: Codex (GPT-5.4) <codex@openai.com>

* Harden sanitizer: block svg/math/base, restrict data: to img[src], dedup BuildFallbackHtmlAsync

- Add svg, math, foreignobject, use, base to disallowed elements to close
  XSS vectors from SVG/MathML namespaces and base-tag URL hijacking
- Restrict data: URI allowance to img[src] only, preventing phishing via
  data:text/html on anchor hrefs
- Extract duplicated BuildFallbackHtmlAsync into HtmlSanitizer to prevent
  future divergence between ImapSyncService and McpTools
- Bump CurrentPolicyVersion to 3 to trigger lazy resanitization

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex (GPT-5.4) <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
tests: fix worksheet fixture regeneration (#334)

Regenerate worksheet fixtures in a temporary directory outside the package
tree, then copy the deterministic XML and extracted test outputs back into the
expected directories. This avoids GAPDoc path resolution failures while
regenerating worksheets that use chunks.

Also run regen_tests.g in CI and fail if it crashes or changes
tracked fixtures.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(domain): stamp input reads with last update time

Summary:
- stamp domain process-image entries with their last update time and
  expose last valid/invalid cycle timestamps in domain diagnostics
- change EtherCAT.read_input/2 to return %{value, updated_at_us}
  and remove the extra read_input_sample API surface
- update docs, examples, and tests to match the new input-read
  contract and richer domain cycle telemetry

Rationale:
- generic PDO reads are ETS-backed cached values, so callers need an
  honest freshness signal without pretending to have exact edge time
- precise timing remains a DC latch concern; normal input reads now
  report last update time while keeping the public surface small

Validation:
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
docs(plans): close completed alignment plans

Summary:
- move the SyncManager/domain and Distributed Clocks alignment plans
  from `active/` to `completed/`
- update the execution-plan index and roadmap so they no longer treat
  those child plans as unfinished work
- move the genuinely unresolved items into the tech-debt tracker and
  keep the completed-plan cross-references consistent

Rationale:
- these plans describe work that has already landed in the current
  library line
- keeping them active makes the docs overstate unfinished execution and
  hides the real remaining work
- debt such as sync-sensitive CoE-mode hardware proof belongs in the
  tracker, not in artificially open execution plans

Validation:
- mix docs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs(repo): clean stale docs entry paths

Summary:
- remove stale notebook and harness-doctor references from the public
  README and docs spine
- move the superseded narrow DC SYNC1/latch plan out of `active/`
  into `completed/`
- align the roadmap and quality docs with the current maintained
  script-based hardware surface

Rationale:
- the docs tree should describe the repo that actually exists, not
  removed Livebooks or nonexistent Mix tasks
- active plan indices should only list work that still changes behavior
- new users need a clear, script-first entry path instead of stale
  interactive-harness guidance

Validation:
- mix docs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(dc): expose lock contract in status snapshots

Summary:
- add `await_lock?` and `lock_policy` to `%EtherCAT.DC.Status{}`
- populate the status fields from both the live DC worker and the
  master's inactive/disabled fallback snapshot
- fix the top-level `EtherCAT.dc_status/0` docs/spec to match the real
  lifecycle semantics and add regression coverage

Rationale:
- diagnostics and tooling need to see both the startup gate and the
  runtime lock-loss policy without peeking into hidden config
- status snapshots should describe the configured DC contract even when
  the runtime is inactive
- the public wrapper should document the actual distinction between an
  idle disabled session and a missing master process

Validation:
- mix format lib/ethercat/dc/status.ex lib/ethercat/dc.ex
  lib/ethercat/master.ex lib/ethercat.ex
  test/ethercat/dc/runtime_test.exs test/ethercat/master_test.exs
  test/ethercat_test.exs
- mix test test/ethercat_test.exs test/ethercat/dc/runtime_test.exs
  test/ethercat/master_test.exs
- mix test
- mix docs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(dc): make runtime lock policy explicit

Summary:
- add `lock_policy` to `%EtherCAT.DC.Config{}` and validate it in
  high-level master config
- separate activation-time DC lock gating from runtime lock-loss handling
  in `EtherCAT.Master`
- remove Livebooks and point examples/docs at maintained scripts instead

Rationale:
- startup `await_lock?` and runtime lock loss are different concerns and
  need different policy controls
- the public docs and active plans should describe the DC contract that
  the code now actually implements
- maintained scripts are the supported hardware surface; keeping stale
  notebooks around teaches the wrong API and runtime model

Validation:
- mix format lib/ethercat.ex lib/ethercat/dc/config.ex
  lib/ethercat/master.ex lib/ethercat/master/config.ex
  test/ethercat/master/config_test.exs test/ethercat/master_test.exs
- mix test
- mix docs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(content): expand push-up and pull-up exercise libraries (#26)

Summary:
- Expand push-up progression content with many additional variants and
  prerequisites in the shared flagship content pack.
- Add a full pull-up skill tree with beginner-to-advanced exercise variants
  and include it in content manifest metadata.
- Mirror expanded push-up/pull-up trees in API-served content and add richer
  push-up and pull-up mobile training sessions with expanded exercise lists.
- Update api-client tests to reflect the new pull-up tree in local manifest
  expectations.

Rationale:
- The issue requires substantially broader push-up and pull-up exercise
  coverage so users can progress across more movement variants.
- Keeping shared content, API responses, and mobile session library aligned
  prevents drift between recommendation data and surfaced session exercises.

Tests:
- pnpm turbo run lint --filter=@cedar/api-client --filter=@cedar/api --filter=@cedar/mobile
- pnpm turbo run test --filter=@cedar/api-client --filter=@cedar/api --filter=@cedar/mobile
- pnpm turbo run build --filter=@cedar/api-client --filter=@cedar/api --filter=@cedar/mobile

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(examples): add split-sm recovery validation

Summary:
- add `--split-sm` mode to `examples/fault_tolerance.exs` so the
  maintained recovery script can exercise split `{domain, SyncManager}`
  reconnect flows
- extend the script to track multiple domains, verify post-recovery
  domain state, and confirm split attachments are restored on the
  reconnected slaves
- update the examples index and SyncManager/domain plan to mark the
  maintained split-SM recovery scenario as landed

Rationale:
- split attachment support should be proven through a maintained
  recovery script, not only through unit coverage and the multi-domain
  performance example
- this moves the remaining gap in the SyncManager/domain plan from
  script design to hardware execution

Validation:
- elixir -e '"examples/fault_tolerance.exs" |> File.read!() |> Code.string_to_quoted!() |> then(fn _ -> IO.puts("ok") end)'
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs(api): align public lifecycle guidance

Summary:
- update top-level `EtherCAT` and `Master` docs to describe the
  current phase, waiter, and domain-config contracts
- clarify that high-level domain configs are intent-only and that the
  master allocates logical windows automatically
- mark the lifecycle spec alignment review as historical analysis and
  keep it indexed as rationale for the master-owned recovery model

Rationale:
- the public API docs should match the runtime behavior now exposed to
  users, especially around `:recovering` and the current wait semantics
- older design reviews are still useful, but they should not read as if
  they describe the live implementation unchanged

Validation:
- mix test
- mix docs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs(examples): refresh runtime model guidance

Summary:
- add `docs/references/` and the EtherCAT spec index to `AGENTS.md`
- update README, examples, and Livebooks to describe the current
  whole-millisecond scheduling contract and master-owned logical window
  allocation
- align fault-tolerance and multi-domain example wording with the
  public `:recovering` phase

Rationale:
- the public docs and notebook scaffolding should match the runtime
  model that now exists instead of older startup and logical-base
  assumptions
- stale wording in examples is one of the easiest ways to reintroduce
  removed config fields and outdated lifecycle expectations

Validation:
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

CED-30: Improve mobile tree navigation and node detail UX (#23)

* feat(mobile): split tree and detail views for smaller screens

Summary:
- Make tree view the default and move node details into a separate view.
- Add searchable node navigation that recenters the canvas and applies
  a visible focus glow to the matched node.
- Allow locked nodes to be selected so users can inspect prerequisites,
  including a horizontal breadcrumb path to the selected node.
- Increase key touch target sizes for better mobile usability.

Rationale:
- Mobile users could not reliably navigate dense tree and detail content in
  one stacked layout.
- Separating views and adding search-to-focus reduces navigation friction
  while keeping progression context visible.

Tests:
- pnpm --filter @cedar/mobile lint
- pnpm --filter @cedar/mobile test
- pnpm --filter @cedar/mobile build

feat(mobile): add in-session exercise player flow (#25)

Summary:
- add a session player to the mobile skill-tree screen with rounds, sets,
  between-set rest, and between-round rest controls
- implement session phase state for active sets, timed rests, and timed-set
  auto-advance so timed exercises start automatically at set/break transitions
- add in-session quick time controls, current exercise context, next exercise
  hinting, and a quick exercise detail overlay that preserves timer state

Rationale:

  browsing, including adjustable timings while the session is live
- users need current/next guidance and fast drill-down detail access without
  resetting workout timing or losing session progress

- suggest a session from selected skill nodes and expose session access in
  the node detail panel
- render session library entries and exercise details in the mobile UI and
  cover session suggestion behavior with tests

  rebuilt in place

  mappings
- refresh the SyncManager/domain alignment plan to reflect landed work
  and remaining recovery proof

Rationale:
- master-owned recovery needs a terminal fallback for faults that are not
  retryable without rebuilding shared cyclic resources
- split attachment support is now core behavior, so reconnect coverage
  should prove per-domain cache reuse explicitly
- the active plan had drifted behind the implementation and was no longer
  a reliable guide for the remaining work

commit.message:
refactor(recovery): make runtime faults master-owned

Summary:
- add a dedicated internal recovery state in the master for runtime faults
- move slave reconnect from self-heal polling to explicit master authorization
- route DC runtime and lock changes into the lifecycle boundary and add the refactor roadmap doc

Rationale:
- runtime recovery should be owned by the master instead of being split between startup degraded logic and slave-local reconnect code
- this keeps initial plan ownership in the master while domains, slaves, and DC keep their live runtime state
- making DC and reconnect faults cross the process boundary is the needed base for later selective rebuild work

Validation:
- mix test
- attempted live validation with examples/scan.exs and fault_tolerance.exs --skip-hardware on enp0s31f6, but the bus timed out during startup

Summary:
- Added CED-28 QA report with explicit validation and acceptance evidence.
- Added small and large mobile viewport screenshots from the exported app.
- Documented final epic acceptance re-check outcomes for CED-23.

Rationale:
- Preserve reproducible evidence for final sign-off and release traceability.
- Provide visual artifacts directly tied to the redesigned skill tree experience.

fix(mobile): polish tree readability and touch interactions (#20)

Summary:
- Tune canvas gesture responder capture to require intentional movement,
  preserving node taps while keeping pan/pinch behavior.
- Improve compact mobile layout spacing, touch target sizing, and legend
  wrapping for narrower screens.
- Prevent node label clipping by using two-line titles and dedicated state
  pills, and make detail content scrollable within a bounded panel.

Rationale:
- The redesign introduced dense mobile states where node text and detail
  content could become hard to read or feel cramped.
- Tight gesture capture was competing with taps; movement thresholds make
  interaction intent clearer without regressing navigation gestures.
- Isolating state copy from the node circle avoids visual clipping while
  keeping status transitions readable.

feat(mobile): render connected branch-style prerequisite lines (#19)

Summary:
- Replace straight edge rendering with multi-segment branch connectors that
  route from node-border terminals through connected joints.
- Precompute connector geometry from tree edges to keep pan/zoom rendering
  smooth while preserving full prerequisite coverage.
- Apply locked/available/completed visual states to connector thickness,
  glow, and opacity to strengthen progression cues.

Rationale:
- Straight center-to-center lines created clutter and weaker hierarchy in
  branching paths.
- Branch-style connectors preserve clear relation flow and end near node
  borders, improving legibility around dense nodes.

fix: publish missing production api routes via vercel root handlers (#6)

* fix(api): add vercel root fallbacks for astro api routes

Summary:
- Added Vercel root `api/*` handlers for health, agent run/status,
  checkout, portal, and Stripe webhook routes.
- Reused existing Astro endpoint logic by bridging Node req/res to Web
  Request/Response objects in a shared adapter helper.
- Disabled body parsing for the Stripe webhook fallback route to keep
  raw payload verification intact.

Rationale:
- Production currently serves root `api/*` functions, while these
  six business-critical routes only existed under `src/pages/api/*`.
- Adding root fallbacks guarantees these endpoints are published even
  when deployment is running in static + root API mode.
- Delegating to Astro handlers avoids duplicate business logic and
  keeps behavior aligned between both runtime paths.

Tests:
- npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(api): move bridge helper outside vercel api routes

Summary:
- Moved the request/response bridge helper from `api/_lib` to
  `src/server/vercel/astro-bridge.ts`.
- Updated all root API fallback handlers to import the relocated
  helper path.

Rationale:
- Files under `api/` can be treated as Vercel function entries.
- Keeping the helper under `api/_lib` risks build-time function
  entry confusion and deployment failures.
- Moving helper code outside `api/` preserves behavior while avoiding
  accidental endpoint registration.

Tests:
- npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(api): rewrite missing api routes to fallback handlers

Summary:
- Moved root fallback handlers to `api/fallback/*` paths.
- Added `vercel.json` rewrites from business endpoints to fallback
  handlers for health, agent run/status, checkout, portal, and
  stripe webhook routes.
- Updated imports in fallback handlers to keep delegation to shared
  Astro endpoint logic.

Rationale:
- Publishing duplicate top-level API paths can trigger deployment
  routing conflicts.
- Rewrites keep public paths stable while mapping execution to
  dedicated fallback handlers.
- This keeps the production fix explicit and isolated from existing
  Astro route declarations.

Tests:
- npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(api): remove astro sentry dependency from health fallback

Summary:
- Replaced delegated `/api/health` fallback call with a direct Node
  handler implementation.
- Kept behavior parity for success/failure responses and synthetic
  `?fail=1` path.
- Wired error capture to server-side Sentry extension for parity with
  runtime reporting.

Rationale:
- Delegating health fallback to Astro's route pulled in
  `@sentry/astro`, which is not required for root Vercel function
  bundling and can break deployment packaging.
- Keeping the fallback self-contained avoids that packaging failure.

Tests:
- npx esbuild api/fallback/health.ts --bundle --platform=node --format=esm
- npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Honor context mode in runner phase detection

Use SourceContext.mode in runWith() and runWithSync() when
deciding whether phase one requires a second parse pass. This
keeps mode-aware contexts aligned with isStaticContext() and
avoids probing or forcing two-pass execution when mode:
"static" is declared.

Add regression tests for empty-annotation static contexts in
both async and sync runner paths, and update the extension and
runner docs to clarify that explicit mode hints also control
second-pass behavior.

commit.message:
refactor(master): internalize domain logical address planning

Summary:
- remove logical_base from the public Domain.Config API
- introduce an internal master domain plan that carries logical_base
- keep automatic logical window allocation inside master config
- update examples and docs to reflect master-owned address planning

Rationale:
- align the high-level API with the EtherCAT model where the master owns
  logical address space and slaves program FMMUs from that plan
- keep logical_base only on the low-level Domain.start_link/1 path where
  callers bypass the master and need direct control
- separate user intent from the compiled runtime plan to make the API
  cleaner and harder to misuse

Validation:
- mix test
- mix run examples/multi_domain.exs --interface enp0s31f6 --run-s 0
  --cross-samples 1

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Fix annotation shape and wrapper reuse paths

injectAnnotations() now preserves built-in object state shapes (Date,
Map, Set, RegExp) and avoids flattening non-plain objects into plain
object spreads.

Wrapper unwrapping logic is centralized in annotations.ts via
unwrapInjectedAnnotationWrapper(), and parser/modifiers now reuse the
shared helper to avoid drift.

multiple() and passThrough() now avoid redundant array cloning by
attaching inherited annotations to known-fresh arrays in place.

Add regression coverage for injectAnnotations() shape handling and
non-plain object mutation safety.

commit.message:
Add cedar-style background art for mobile tree canvas (#16)

* feat(mobile): add cedar background art for tree canvas

Summary:
- Generate and add a cedar-style tree canvas background PNG asset.
- Layer the asset into the mobile canvas viewport behind nodes and edges.
- Add a dark tint overlay to preserve contrast for light node labels.

Rationale:
- The tree canvas needed production-ready environmental art that stays calm
  behind connectors and cards without introducing logos or text.
- A subdued textured background improves visual depth while maintaining
  high readability across canvas interactions.

Tests:
- pnpm --filter @cedar/mobile lint
- pnpm --filter @cedar/mobile test
- pnpm --filter @cedar/mobile build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(mobile): clean up tree canvas background image typing

Summary:
- Remove unsupported pointer-events prop from the tree canvas background
  Image component.
- Add a PNG module declaration for mobile TypeScript asset imports.

Rationale:
- The background art integration must compile cleanly across Expo/React Native
  targets without invalid prop typing.
- Explicit PNG typing keeps asset imports predictable in the mobile workspace.

Tests:
- pnpm --filter @cedar/mobile lint
- pnpm --filter @cedar/mobile test
- pnpm --filter @cedar/mobile build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Stabilize multiple() suggest annotation handling

Align wrapper-shape checks across parser and modifiers by reusing a shared
annotationWrapperKeys constant from annotations.ts.

multiple().suggest() now attempts annotation-aware suggest with inherited
state and then retries with unwrapped primitive state when needed. This
keeps annotation-aware suggest flows working while preserving primitive
state expectations.

inheritAnnotations() no longer mutates extensible non-plain objects,
preventing annotation leakage through shared initialState instances.

Add regression coverage for primitive suggest fallback and non-plain
state leakage scenarios.

https://github.com/dahlia/optique/pull/146#discussion_r2901947249
https://github.com/dahlia/optique/pull/146#discussion_r2901953340
https://github.com/dahlia/optique/pull/146#discussion_r2901953345

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(local): add markdown task loop and Chinese docs

Summary:
- add local Markdown task tracking with inbox, archive, state,
  and results directories
- expose task_update for local runs, autoload .env beside the
  workflow, and generalize the UI/config summaries
- add local closed-loop examples plus a full Chinese doc set and
  README entrypoints

Rationale:
- make the smallest self-hosted closed loop work without Jira so
  local task files can drive Codex runs directly
- reduce first-run friction by making the documented .env flow
  work out of the box and by documenting both English and Chinese
  paths

Tests:
- gofmt -w .
- GOPROXY=https://proxy.golang.org,direct go test ./...

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: symphony-go

commit.message:
Increase BYOClaw divider gap by roughly one line height

Signed-off-by: Codex (GPT 5) <codex@openai.com>

feat: add source-aware workspace branch naming

Summary:
- add workspace.linear_branch_scope for Linear branch naming
- derive GitHub branch scope from tracker.repo and issue number
- prepare deterministic work branches before agent execution
- update workflow prompts and tests for the new branch format

Rationale:
- make branch names identify the task source and project scope at a glance
- avoid relying on prompts alone for branch naming consistency
- keep Linear and GitHub branch formats stable without cross-source coupling

Tests:
- go test ./internal/config ./internal/workspace
- go test ./...

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Nudge BYOClaw spacing slightly further from divider

Signed-off-by: Codex (GPT 5) <codex@openai.com>

Propagate annotations into multiple suggestions

multiple().suggest() now inherits annotations from the outer multiple
state into the inner parser initial state before calling suggest().

This makes annotation-aware inner suggest() implementations behave
consistently with parse()/complete() annotation propagation.

Adds sync and async regression tests for annotation visibility in inner
suggest state.

https://github.com/dahlia/optique/pull/146#discussion_r2901938233

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Retry unwrapped state after complete failure

multiple() now retries complete() with an unwrapped primitive state when
the first completion attempt returns { success: false } on an injected
wrapper.

This aligns behavior with the existing throw-based fallback and prevents
annotation-enabled regressions for primitive inner states.

Adds sync and async regression tests that fail first on wrapped object
state, then succeed on unwrapped primitive state.

https://github.com/dahlia/optique/pull/146#discussion_r2901938869

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>
